Title: Multiomics analyses of the effects of LED white light on the ripening of apricot fruits

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in LED-treated postharvest fruit flesh at 8 d
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in LED-treated postharvest fruit flesh at 8 d
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light, 5 μmol m−2 s−1, 12 h light/12 h dark, 20 °C postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant metabolism associated with delayed ripening and quality maintenance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED-induced USP upregulation was associated with activation of ascorbate and aldarate metabolism and altered uronate/ascorbate metabolite accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. USP was upregulated under LED irradiation in integrated transcriptome-metabolome analysis. 2. USP participates in synthesis of D-glucuronate from UDP-D-glucose. 3. Its induction was associated with antioxidant-related metabolic remodeling during delayed postharvest ripening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential postharvest metabolic engineering target to enhance antioxidant metabolism and shelf life under light-based storage strategies
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated RNA-seq and metabolite pathway mapping under LED vs dark treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as a metabolism-associated DEG rather than directly transgenically validated.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One AO decreased and another AO increased under LED at 8 d
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially regulated in LED-treated postharvest fruit flesh
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light, 5 μmol m−2 s−1, 12 h light/12 h dark, 20 °C postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and redox homeostasis during fruit storage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED altered AO expression and was associated with decreased L-dehydroascorbate accumulation and delayed senescence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AO genes responded differentially to LED treatment. 2. Combined with APX changes, AO remodeling altered the ascorbate/aldarate pathway. 3. The net effect was a decrease in L-dehydroascorbate accumulation under LED.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for tuning postharvest antioxidant status in apricot fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated RNA-seq and metabolite pathway mapping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses AO in the ascorbate pathway context rather than as a transcriptional regulator.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Several POD genes were responsive to LED; PODA2/POD4/POD31/POD42 affected depending on comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in postharvest fruit flesh under LED and associated with oxidation and phenylpropanoid pathways
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to oxidative balance and cell wall/quality maintenance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED-modulated POD expression was linked to phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and maintenance of apricot fruit quality during storage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple POD genes changed expression under LED treatment. 2. Integrated analysis linked POD to phenylpropanoid biosynthesis with altered chromatin accessibility. 3. POD, together with 4CL, was implicated in regulation of lignin-related metabolites and quality preservation.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Associated with altered chromatin accessibility in ATAC-seq
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate biomarker for postharvest quality maintenance and antioxidant-related shelf-life improvement
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis plus integrated ATAC-seq/metabolomics pathway association
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as a common functional gene because the paper emphasizes antioxidant/quality association rather than a single terminal biosynthetic step.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest at 0 d, decreased during storage, and lower in LED than CK at 8 d
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in LED-treated fruit during ripening
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED suppressed SUC expression, consistent with altered sugar-related flavor metabolism during delayed ripening
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUC was among the LED-regulated genes affecting flavor and aroma. 2. Its expression decreased more strongly under LED than in dark storage. 3. The change was associated with postharvest sugar metabolism remodeling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate postharvest sweetness and ripening-related sugar transport
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis across storage treatments
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter-type core gene, not a pathway enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in LED-8 d relative to CK-8 d and increased during storage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: LED promoted expression toward the end of storage
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED promoted ACS expression while still delaying fruit ripening, indicating altered ethylene-associated ripening control
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS was identified as a key rate-limiting enzyme in ethylene biosynthesis. 2. Its expression was upregulated about 2.2-fold in LED-treated fruit relative to CK-8 d. 3. Despite increased ACS expression, LED delayed ripening, indicating complex postharvest regulation.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter accessibility and TF motif context implicated by ATAC-seq upstream regulatory analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for postharvest ripening management under light treatments, but requires caution due to context-dependent relation to ripening delay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potentially promotes ethylene biosynthesis despite delayed ripening outcome
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression profiling; integrated ATAC-seq motif analysis; physiological ripening assessment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, the paper frames ACS primarily as a ripening-associated common target rather than the nutrient-metabolism focus.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ACC oxidase family, non-heme iron oxygenase
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in LED-8 d relative to CK-8 d and increased during storage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: LED promoted expression toward the end of storage
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED promoted ACO1 expression while delaying ripening, indicating complex ethylene signaling in postharvest response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACO1 converts ACC to ethylene in ripening fruit. 2. LED increased ACO1 expression about 2.3-fold relative to CK-8 d. 3. ACO1 was highlighted as a notable LED-responsive gene in delayed postharvest ripening.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter accessibility and ERF motif enrichment implicated by ATAC-seq
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for monitoring ethylene-related responses under postharvest light management
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potentially increases ethylene pathway activity despite delayed visual ripening
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression profiling; integrated motif analysis; physiological phenotype association
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is repeatedly emphasized in Results and Discussion as a key LED-responsive ripening gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step into flavonoid/anthocyanin branch from phenylpropanoid-derived cinnamoyl-CoA/p-coumaroyl-CoA pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses a phenylpropanoid CoA ester with malonyl-CoA units to form chalcone precursors for downstream flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and initiates pathway toward flavonoids and anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: LED-responsive; CHS genes highlighted as upregulated in integrated flavonoid pathway analysis, though CHS2 showed comparison-dependent changes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in LED-treated fruit flesh during storage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity, color maintenance, quality preservation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED-modulated CHS expression promoted flavonoid pathway activation and anthocyanin biosynthesis-related changes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS was identified as a key enzyme in flavonoid biosynthesis under LED treatment. 2. Increased cinnamoyl-CoA under LED was associated with CHS upregulation. 3. CHS induction altered pinobanksin 3-acetate and chrysin levels and promoted downstream DFR expression and anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Likely influenced by LED-responsive chromatin accessibility and MYB TF enrichment; no direct locus-specific epigenetic proof for CHS provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for postharvest color and antioxidant trait engineering or marker-assisted improvement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter flavonoid composition rather than uniformly increasing all flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated transcriptome-metabolome pathway analysis; differential expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to CHS, CHS2, and CHS3 in different contexts; CHS is the focal enzyme family-level signal.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Leucoanthocyanidins / reduced flavonoid intermediates leading to epigallocatechin and anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to corresponding leucoanthocyanidin intermediates in flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for anthocyanin-related products and promotes branch flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under LED according to integrated flavonoid pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DFR upregulation under LED promoted epigallocatechin accumulation and stimulated anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR was induced downstream of CHS under LED treatment. 2. DFR upregulation promoted epigallocatechin accumulation. 3. The enzyme contributed to activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis during delayed ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing antioxidant flavonoids and maintaining fruit color under storage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux among flavonoid subclasses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes DFR as part of LED-induced flavonoid remodeling.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to caffeoyl-CoA in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Modifies phenylpropanoid intermediates contributing to phenolic acid and lignin-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased under LED treatment according to abstract
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in LED-treated fruit at 8 d
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LED-induced CCOAOMT1 expression was associated with accumulation of caffeoyl quinic acid and activation of phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCOAOMT1 was listed among the key genes increased by LED treatment. 2. Its induction coincided with accumulation of caffeoyl quinic acid. 3. The gene contributed to LED activation of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance phenolic antioxidant stability in postharvest fruits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in the abstract as one of the notable LED-induced pathway genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups between CoA esters and shikimate/quinate intermediates in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for caffeoyl quinic acid and lignin-related products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced chromatin openness with decreased expression in one integrated analysis; later described as increased open chromatin but significantly downregulated expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated under LED-related integrated analysis despite chromatin changes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Caffeoyl quinic acid; caffeoyl shikimic acid; lignin-related phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altered HCT expression under LED changed p-coumaroyl shikimic acid, caffeoyl quinic acid, and caffeoyl shikimic acid accumulation, contributing to quality maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT was a major integrated omics node in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis. 2. LED altered both chromatin accessibility and HCT transcript abundance. 3. HCT changes affected caffeoyl quinic acid-related metabolite accumulation and lignin-associated downstream metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ATAC-seq showed altered chromatin accessibility at HCT-associated regions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering phenolic acid composition and storage quality, but regulatory complexity should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Conflicting accessibility-expression relationship suggests context-dependent regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper contains a wording inconsistency regarding whether HCT open chromatin increased or decreased; downregulated expression is clear.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry to hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to their CoA thioesters for downstream phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin and flavonoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in phenylpropanoid entry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased open chromatin with downregulated expression in one integrated analysis; later described as both significantly upregulated and downregulated across loci/comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: LED altered 4CL-associated accessibility and transcript levels during storage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylpropanoid-derived lignin-related metabolites; caffeoyl quinic acid-associated flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 4CL responded to LED and, together with HCT and POD, contributed to regulation of phenylpropanoid flux associated with storage quality
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL was identified in integrated transcriptomic and ATAC-seq analyses as a phenylpropanoid-related gene. 2. LED altered its chromatin accessibility and expression. 3. Together with HCT and POD, 4CL was linked to regulation of lignin-related metabolites and fruit quality preservation.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ATAC-seq indicated altered chromatin accessibility around 4CL-associated regions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to tune phenylpropanoid flux for postharvest quality retention
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression behavior appears locus- or comparison-dependent in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains some inconsistency in directionality, so only the integrated phenylpropanoid role is captured.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene signal transduction associated with postharvest ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of ACO1 and ACS
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of ethylene signaling; likely feedforward in ripening-associated transcription
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in DAR motif analysis under LED treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter motif enrichment in LED-treated fruit at 8 d
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERF2 enrichment under LED was linked to ACS/ACO1 activation and delayed senescence regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of ethylene-responsive ripening under LED treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF2 motifs were enriched in LED-responsive open chromatin regions. 2. ERF2 was highlighted as acting downstream of ethylene signaling. 3. The study states that ERF2 enrichment promoted ACO1 and ACS expression in apricot fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Supported indirectly by ATAC-seq promoter accessibility enrichment of ERF motifs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for postharvest ripening control via light/ethylene interaction
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May promote ethylene-pathway genes despite delayed ripening phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif enrichment analysis integrated with expression changes of target genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Derived from motif analysis rather than direct TF perturbation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis and fruit color maintenance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in LED-treated fruits according to ATAC-seq motif analysis and discussion
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYB124 enrichment under LED likely upregulated CHS3 and contributed to maintenance of fruit color
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Light-responsive activator of flavonoid pathway entry gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB124 was enriched in LED-treated apricot fruit. 2. The paper proposes that this enrichment upregulated CHS3 expression. 3. MYB124 likely contributed to maintenance of fruit color through anthocyanin-related biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Inferred through ATAC-seq motif enrichment in accessible chromatin regions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for improving postharvest color retention and flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter pigmentation and broader MYB-regulated stress pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif enrichment plus expression association discussed in integrated analysis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the MYB specifically linked to color maintenance in the discussion, distinct from other MYBs listed as DEGs.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: LED-responsive ripening regulation associated with quality-related metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in CK-8 d vs LED-8 d and showed same trend as ACS, ACO1, CHS2 in LED-regulated DEG set
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in LED-treated fruit at 8 d than CK-8 d
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: bZIP44 was a notable LED-responsive TF associated with delayed ripening and metabolic reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller inferred from repeated LED responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP44 was one of the few TF DEGs detected in CK-8 d vs LED-8 d. 2. Its expression was higher in LED-treated fruit. 3. The gene was also included among the 12 LED-regulated DEGs with a consistent trend during ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory marker for light-responsive postharvest ripening control
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function on specific metabolic targets was not directly resolved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression profiling across storage treatments
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because it is repeatedly emphasized among LED-regulated TFs, although direct downstream metabolic targets were not identified.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: LED-responsive ripening-related regulation affecting fruit quality metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in CK-0 d vs LED-8 d
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under LED treatment during storage
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LED white light during postharvest storage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TCP factors were among the notable LED-affected TFs associated with delayed ripening and quality changes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP2, TCP4 and TCP15 were upregulated under LED treatment. 2. The abstract highlighted TCP among notable genes affected by LED. 3. These TFs likely participate in transcriptional reprogramming during delayed ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory markers for light-responsive postharvest quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct metabolic targets were not resolved in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as a TF group rather than individually functionally validated regulators.
